summary: Fetch-store pipeline for all source types: owns the Fetcher interface, Service struct, and two parallel refresh paths (Telegram/generic Source)

requires: Channels pre-seeded in DB before refresh runs
guarantees: Returns RefreshResult with per-channel error list — never propagates a single-channel failure as a top-level error
Sync state checkpoint is updated only when at least one message was fetched
Duplicate messages silently skipped (relies on DB UNIQUE constraint)

state: Stateless after construction; Service holds immutable fetcher, store reference, and limit

observations.text: RefreshAll computes maxID by scanning the full returned slice, not relying on sorted order — correct but O(n) per channel
RefreshFiltered ignores its sourceNames argument entirely and delegates to RefreshAll — callers expecting selective refresh get a full refresh silently
RefreshSources falls back from numeric ExternalID parse failure to crc32.ChecksumIEEE — non-integer IDs (RSS GUIDs, Reddit post IDs) are hashed into the TelegramMsgID field, risking collision

Two parallel refresh paths exist: Fetcher-based (Telegram MTProto, integer message IDs) and Source-based (generic interface, opaque string checkpoints). RefreshAllSources delegates to RefreshSources which calls src.FetchMessages rather than the Fetcher.

Deduplication is entirely delegated to the DB UNIQUE constraint on Messages; if Create returns an error it is silently skipped regardless of cause — storage errors during message write are indistinguishable from duplicates
scope.json hint confirmed: storage called ~8 times per channel in RefreshAll (List, SyncState.Get, Messages.Create×N, SyncState.Upsert) — storage is the dominant dependency
